mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.0%, weak labor market conditions signal deteriorating consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in over 55% of periods where UNRATE exceeds 7.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic strength, increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 60% of historical instances, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: During periods where industrial production remains below its 2007 peak, supply-side constraints limit corporate output growth, causing negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO levels below 100.0 do not predict negative 5-day returns, or the mechanism shows positive predictive power during the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening employment conditions, which support consumer demand and drive positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across rolling 2-year windows, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI exceeds 235.0, rising inflation expectations force monetary authorities to consider tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 65% of periods where CPIAUCSL crosses 235.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between housing starts and retail sales indicates sectoral rotation; when housing starts rise while retail sales fall, equity markets price in economic fragility and decline.
failure_condition: The HOUST-RSAFS divergence fails to predict negative 20-day returns, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: During post-crisis recovery phases, declining unemployment combined with steady payroll growth confirms sustainable expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal produces negative 5-day returns in over 55% of recovery-phase observations, or predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward validation.
